successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and LeaveRequest matching the specified structure.
src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports LeaveRequestRepository with create, findById, findByEmployeeId, and update methods.
src/modules/leave/postgresql-leave.repository.ts exists and exports PostgreSqlLeaveRequestRepository implementing LeaveRequestRepository.
A PostgreSQL leave_requests table schema is defined with columns id, employee_id, leave_type, start_date, end_date, status, created_at, and updated_at.
Vitest tests cover repository create, findById, findByEmployeeId, and update behavior.
All LeaveRequest persistence access is routed through LeaveRequestRepository and no persistence contract is exposed outside the repository implementation.
Project architecture documentation is updated to describe LeaveRequest, LeaveType values (ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY), and LeaveRequestStatus values (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED).

constraints: Limit implementation to LeaveRequest persistence, type definitions, repository contract, PostgreSQL repository implementation, schema definition, tests, and related documentation.
Do not implement submitLeaveRequest, approveLeaveRequest, rejectLeaveRequest, notifications, balance deductions, or workflow logic.
Use the repository pattern: all database access must go through LeaveRequestRepository.
All async repository operations must handle errors; no unhandled promise rejections.
Any state-changing persistence operations must support the project's audit-record requirement when audit infrastructure becomes available; do not bypass audit architecture.
No sensitive data may be logged.

Preserve modular-monolith dependency rules; this phase introduces ownership within the leave module only and must not create reverse dependencies from employee, policy, balance, or notification modules.
Use TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and Vitest consistent with project standards.
outOfScope: Fastify API endpoints for leave submission, approval, rejection, or balance queries.
Employee, policy, balance, or notification module changes beyond dependency-safe type references.
